_comment: APIs useful for forecasting demand, foot traffic, and operations for a dollar-store chain (e.g. Dollar Tree). Each entry under forecast_7d lists only the endpoints that return FORWARD-LOOKING data for the next ~7 days.

weather._comment: Weather directly drives impulse buys (umbrellas, fans, hand warmers, rain ponchos) and foot traffic volume. Cold/rain suppresses visits; heat waves spike beverage/fan sales.

apis.dollar_store_use: Per-store 7-day forecast: stock rain gear before a storm, push fans before a heat wave, anticipate traffic dips on rain days.
Higher resolution than NWS (hourly). Use weather_code to categorize storm severity. Good for same-week staffing and reorder triggers.
Probabilistic forecast: if 35 of 40 members show rain Saturday, stock umbrellas now. Reduces over/under-stocking vs. single-model forecasts.
High AQI days → spike in mask, air-freshener, and allergy-relief sales. Pre-position inventory near stores in wildfire-prone regions.
Trigger emergency stock alerts: a 'Winter Storm Warning' should auto-flag stores to front-load salt, shovels, candles, batteries.
Stores near rivers: flood risk forecast drives demand for buckets, mops, tarps, bottled water. Also affects store accessibility.

events._comment: Events cause predictable foot-traffic surges near stores. A stadium game 2 miles from a Dollar Tree spikes snack/drink/party-supply sales for 3–4 hours before and after.

apis.horizon: Any future date range (filter with startDateTime / endDateTime)
apis.dollar_store_use: The broadest event database. Query by lat/lon radius around each store. Concerts, festivals, comedy shows → party supplies, balloons, snacks. Filter by genre to pre-stock accordingly.
Baseball has 162 games/season — near-daily traffic surges near ballparks. Stock team-color party supplies, snacks, drinks on game days. Venue field enables per-store matching.
Covers all 32 NHL teams. Stores near arenas (TD Garden, Madison Square Garden) see pre-game/post-game spikes. Fetch /v1/schedule/{date} for league-wide next-7-day view.
NFL Sundays are the highest single-day traffic spikes near stadiums. 17-week season. Stores near NFL venues should pre-stock party supplies, disposable plates, snacks Thursday–Sunday.
82-game season Oct–Apr. Note: no venue field — infer by home_team.city. Use ESPN basketball/nba scoreboard if arena name is needed for geo-matching.

apis.horizon: Any date range (filter via dates param)
apis.dollar_store_use: MLS season Feb–Nov. Full venue field makes geo-matching easy. Works for querying next 7 days specifically. Free, no auth.
Graduation weekends drive massive dollar-store runs: balloons, banners, plastic cups, tablecloths. PredictHQ covers 1000+ US graduation events with attendance estimates and city-level geo.

mobility._comment: Road conditions affect whether customers can reach the store AND whether delivery trucks can resupply on schedule.

apis.endpoint: GET {state-specific WZDx GeoJSON feed URL}  (MD, NY, WA, DE, LA, ID covered)
apis.horizon: Active + scheduled work zones (start/end dates in GeoJSON properties)
apis.dollar_store_use: If the road fronting a store has a 3-week lane closure starting Thursday, expect 20–40% traffic drop. Flag stores with imminent work zones for adjusted staffing and delivery rerouting.

apis.horizon: Active and scheduled alerts (hours to days)
apis.dollar_store_use: Washington state stores: mountain pass closures affect supply chains. Real-time traffic flow data can signal unexpected traffic surges (detours routing customers past your store).

economics._comment: These APIs are largely LAGGING (historical releases). Included here for context. Not useful for 7-day operational decisions but valuable for quarterly planning.

apis.horizon: LAGGING — most recent release, not a forecast
apis.dollar_store_use: CPI and Consumer Sentiment are leading signals for dollar-store demand trends: high inflation + low sentiment = more customers trading down to dollar stores. Use for monthly/quarterly inventory planning, not 7-day ops.

_not_forward_looking._comment: These services have no 7-day forecast capability. Useful for store site selection, demographics, and historical analysis but NOT for weekly operational decisions.

services.use: Store site selection: median income, population density, poverty rate by census tract. Run once per store or annually.
Monitor Dollar Tree competitors (Dollar General, Five Below) 10-Q/10-K filings for store expansion plans, margin trends, and supply chain disclosures.
Current map data: find competitor locations, bus stops, parking lots near each store. Static — query on demand, not on a 7-day schedule.
Pull historical weather by station for training ML demand models. Not forward-looking.
ERA5 reanalysis back to 1940. Use to correlate past weather with past sales for model training.
CMIP6 projections to 2050. Long-range store mix planning (AC accessories in warming regions).
